evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars for 4 classes across 5 conditions, y-axis 0–100% with percent labels, fully matches brief; figure is unambiguous and readable.
evidence.typography: Script does not set pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) fonts; DejaVu Sans is the default matplotlib font, not a quality journal choice.
evidence.palette: Uses Tableau-10 subset (#4E79A7, #F28E2B, #59A14F, #E15759) which is colorblind-safe for deuteranopia/protanopia, but red (#E15759) and green (#59A14F) are used as categorical class colors—acceptable here but directional discipline not fully observed.
evidence.layout_density: 6×4.2 in figure with well-proportioned bars (width=0.55), legend placed outside right, no overlapping labels, y-range exactly 0–1; space is used efficiently.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and cites specific percentages; legend outside the plot is slightly non-standard for journals but not a blocking issue; Type 3 fonts would trigger a production/copyediting complaint.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type 3 (bitmap) fonts per pdffonts—text is not selectable or editable; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (29 KB), caption.tex (392 B), gen_figure.py (1.5 KB); bonus figure.svg also generated.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains what is shown, names all five conditions and four classes, and provides concrete numerical findings (Class C ~44%/41% in Cond2–3; Class A monotonically rises to 37% in Cond5).

overall_strengths: Clean, well-labeled stacked bar that faithfully implements the brief; y-axis percent formatting and bar proportions are correct.
Caption is specific and quantitative, citing key numerical findings that anchor the reader's interpretation.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') in the script, resulting in Type 3 bitmap fonts in the PDF—text is non-selectable and will fail publisher font-embedding checks.
Default DejaVu Sans font family; for journal submission a more polished sans-serif (e.g., Helvetica/Arial via rcParams or a custom font) is preferred.
